input_row.title: Ensure that use of the 'User Access Administrator' role is restricted
input_row.description: The User Access Administrator role grants the ability to view all resources and manage access assignments at any subscription or management group level within the tenant. Due to its high privilege level, this role assignment should be removed immediately after completing the necessary changes at the root scope to minimize security risks.
input_row.rationale: The User Access Administrator role provides extensive access control privileges. Unnecessary assignments heighten the risk of privilege escalation and unauthorized access. Removing the role immediately after use minimizes security exposure. Impact: Increased administrative effort to manage and remove role assignments appropriately.
input_row.audit: Audit from Azure Portal 1. From Azure Home select the Portal Menu. 2. Select Subscriptions. 3. Select a subscription. 4. Select Access control (IAM). 5. Look for the following banner at the top of the page: Action required: X users have elevated access in your tenant. You should take immediate action and remove all role assignments with elevated access. If the banner is displayed, the User Access Administrator is assigned. Audit from Azure CLI Run the following command: az role assignment list --role "User Access Administrator" --scope "/" Ensure that the command does not return any User Access Administrator role assignment information.
input_row.remediation: Remediate from Azure Portal 1. From Azure Home select the Portal Menu. 2. Select Subscriptions. 3. Select a subscription. 4. Select Access control (IAM). 5. Look for the following banner at the top of the page: Action required: X users have elevated access in your tenant. You should take immediate action and remove all role assignments with elevated access. 6. Click View role assignments. 7. Click Remove. Remediate from Azure CLI Run the following command: az role assignment delete --role "User Access Administrator" --scope "/" References: 1. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/role-based-access-control/built-in-roles 2. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/role-based-access-control/elevate- access-global-admin
input_row.impact: Increased administrative effort to manage and remove role assignments appropriately.

gpt_response:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** azure_role_assignment_user_access_admin_restricted

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**  
The automated program would check:

- **APIs or CLI Commands:** Utilize Azure CLI commands such as:
  - `az role assignment list --role "User Access Administrator" --scope "/"` to list role assignments at the subscription level.
  
- **Resources to enumerate:** The program will check all subscriptions within the Azure tenant.

- **Configuration properties/settings to validate:** The program will check for any active role assignments for the "User Access Administrator" role at any scope level (subscriptions or management groups).

- **Pass/Fail criteria:** 
  - Pass: The command returns no results indicating that there are no User Access Administrator role assignments.
  - Fail: The command returns one or more User Access Administrator role assignments.

- **Example API endpoint or CLI command:** The relevant CLI command is `az role assignment list --role "User Access Administrator" --scope "/"`. The command's output will be analyzed to determine compliance.

This automated approach allows for direct querying of the role assignments, ensuring that the role's use is restricted as per the compliance control requirements.
